Before discussing reciprocity further, let's introduce two important concepts that will run through the rest of this training. For reciprocity and all the other principles of persuasion, there are ways to start a principle operating, and there are ways to make that principle more powerful. We call these activators and amplifiers respectively. An activator is an event or action that causes a principle of persuasion to begin operating. We can use the light switch to symbolize that activators are always there waiting to be turned on to engage the power of the principle we're using. An amplifier, on the other hand, is any event or action that increases the effectiveness of a principle. We can use the dial to symbolize that amplifiers are a way to turn up or increase the power of a principle. Now, each principle has triggers that activate and also amplify its power. We will discuss each principle's activators and amplifiers, but it turns out that the contrast phenomenon amplifies every principle of persuasion. This is why it's so important for you as a sleuth to be prepared to look for all of the contrasts that might be present in any influence situation.
